items.name: Orikon ore
items.examine: Warm amber ore. Hold it to your ear: it is already rehearsing.

items.name: Orikon bar
items.examine: A bar of warm amber metal that holds a note longer than most singers.

items.name: Dawnsteel bar
items.examine: The true-octave metal. The furnace still talks about it.

items.name: Tuning flux
items.examine: A pinch of this and a bar stops arguing with itself.

items.name: Orikon sword
items.examine: An amber blade that rings a clean fifth on the draw.

items.name: Orikon scimitar
items.examine: Curved orikon. Fast enough that the note arrives late.

items.name: Orikon full helm
items.examine: Amber metal, excellent acoustics. Your battle cries gain a chorus.

items.name: Orikon platelegs
items.examine: Orikon legwear. Each step lands on the beat whether you do or not.

items.name: Orikon kiteshield
items.examine: A wall of amber metal. Blows arrive; chords leave.

items.name: Orikon platebody
items.examine: Orikon plate, tuned at the forge. Arrows bounce off in key.

items.name: Dawnsteel platebody
items.examine: First-light metal, struck at the top of the octave. The best plate an anvil will ever sing.

items.name: Dawnsteel full helm
items.examine: Dawnsteel about the ears. Bad ideas sound worse in here, which helps.

items.name: Orikon arrowtips
items.examine: Amber points that hum on the rest.

items.name: Orikon arrow
items.examine: Amber-tipped arrows. They land on the downbeat.

items.name: Longbow (u)
items.examine: A long bow stave. All potential, no string.

items.name: Longbow
items.examine: Slower than a shortbow, but it commits.

items.name: Oak longbow (u)
items.examine: An oak stave shaped for the long draw.

items.name: Oak longbow
items.examine: Oak at full length. The thrum has a baritone now.

items.name: Yew longbow (u)
items.examine: A yew stave as tall as your patience.

items.name: Yew longbow
items.examine: A yew longbow. Takes its time, makes its point.

items.name: Magic longbow (u)
items.examine: A magic stave that is mostly already a bow about it.

items.name: Magic longbow
items.examine: Magic wood at full length. The arrow is a formality; the wait is not.

items.name: Bronze javelin heads
items.examine: Heavy bronze points for the underarm argument.

items.name: Bronze javelin
items.examine: A throwing spear. Diplomacy at medium range.

items.name: Steel javelin heads
items.examine: Steel points, ready for shafts and grudges.

items.name: Steel javelin
items.examine: Steel-tipped and well balanced. Flies straighter than the conversation that caused it.

items.name: Rune javelin heads
items.examine: Storm-grey points with opinions.

items.name: Rune javelin
items.examine: A rune javelin. The last word, thrown.

items.name: Bronze dart tip
items.examine: A small bronze point, eager to be flicked.

items.name: Bronze dart
items.examine: A feathered bronze dart. Quick, cheap, mildly rude.

items.name: Adamant dart tip
items.examine: A green point in a small package.

items.name: Adamant dart
items.examine: An adamant dart. Small, green, and over before the apology.

items.name: Wooden stock
items.examine: A carved crossbow body waiting for its better half.

items.name: Bronze limbs
items.examine: Sprung bronze arms for a crossbow.

items.name: Steel limbs
items.examine: Steel crossbow limbs. They hold a grudge under tension.

items.name: Rune limbs
items.examine: Storm-grey limbs wound tight as a held breath.

items.name: Bronze crossbow (u)
items.examine: Stock and limbs, no string. A promise on a handle.

items.name: Bronze crossbow
items.examine: Point, click, regret (theirs).

items.name: Steel crossbow (u)
items.examine: An unstrung steel crossbow. Quietly confident.

items.name: Steel crossbow
items.examine: A steel crossbow. It does the maths for you.

items.name: Rune crossbow (u)
items.examine: An unstrung rune crossbow, one bowstring from smug.

items.name: Rune crossbow
items.examine: The stavewright's masterpiece. Loads slow, lands like a verdict.

items.name: Bronze bolt tips
items.examine: Stubby bronze points for crossbow bolts.

items.name: Bronze bolts
items.examine: Short, blunt-mannered crossbow ammunition.

items.name: Runite bolt tips
items.examine: Storm-grey bolt points. Briefly airborne lawsuits.

items.name: Runite bolts
items.examine: Runite bolts. They arrive before the twang does.

items.name: Leather cowl
items.examine: A soft leather hood. Mysterious from at least two angles.

items.name: Leather vambraces
items.examine: Keeps the bowstring's opinion off your forearm.

items.name: Leather chaps
items.examine: Soft leather legwear. Quiet on the draw, loud at the tavern.

items.name: Steel studs
items.examine: Small steel domes. Leather's idea of armour school.

items.name: Studded body
items.examine: Leather with steel punctuation.

items.name: Studded chaps
items.examine: Studded legwear. The studs are load-bearing, morale-wise.

items.name: Hard leather
items.examine: Leather tanned until it stopped negotiating.

items.name: Silver ring
items.examine: A bright silver band. Honest work, modest sparkle.

items.name: Silver amulet
items.examine: A silver amulet that catches torchlight and compliments.

items.name: Emerald ring
items.examine: A gold ring set with an emerald, green as fresh envy.

items.name: Emerald amulet
items.examine: An emerald amulet. It approves of your choices, faintly.

items.name: Uncut diamond
items.examine: A rough white stone with an expensive personality underneath.

items.name: Diamond
items.examine: A cut diamond. Light goes in; smugness comes out.

items.name: Diamond ring
items.examine: A gold ring crowned with a diamond. Proposals not included.

items.name: Diamond amulet
items.examine: A diamond amulet that makes the rest of your outfit apologise.

items.name: Uncut dragonstone
items.examine: A rough violet stone, warm to the touch and aware of it.

items.name: Dragonstone
items.examine: A cut dragonstone. It glows like it knows something.

items.name: Dragonstone amulet
items.examine: The best amulet a bench will ever see. It hums the First Chord, off-duty.

items.name: Resonance thread
items.examine: Wool and flax spun in the Weaver's Hush. It holds a pitch and a hem.

items.name: Tuner's apron
items.examine: Scorched, stitched, and earned. The Concord's quiet way of saying maestro.

items.name: Maker's mark
items.examine: A stamped token of the Concord. Proof you made something that rang true.

items.name: Master chisel
items.examine: Yael's pattern. Gems are said to open for it a beat sooner.

items.name: True hammer
items.examine: A hammer forged in key. Smiths swear bars last longer under it.

objs.name: Loom
objs.examine: Warp, weft, and a faint hum. The Hush's pride.

objs.name: Tanning vat
objs.examine: A vat of professional-smelling liquids. The hides go in sceptical and come out leather.

objs.name: Gemcutter's bench
objs.examine: A felt-topped bench scattered with sparkling evidence.

objs.name: Great Bellows-Organ
objs.examine: Six furnaces breathe through it. The forge chord, held since the founding.

npcs.name: Smith-Cantor Branna
npcs.examine: Mistress of the Anvil-Choir. Can tell a flat bar from across the plaza.

npcs.name: Weaver Iseult
npcs.examine: Keeper of the Weaver's Hush. Speaks softly; the loom does the shouting.

npcs.name: Stavewright Orlin
npcs.examine: Runs the Stavewright's Gallery. Judges your draw arm silently.

npcs.name: Gemcutter Yael
npcs.examine: Sees the finished gem inside the rock, and occasionally inside you.

npcs.name: Quartermaster Doram
npcs.examine: Keeps the Concord's ledger of marks and shards. Pays in prestige.

npcs.name: Ore-Broker Halle
npcs.examine: Sells deep-pit ore at plaza prices. Both parties pretend that is fair.

npcs.name: Tanner Mauld
npcs.examine: Smells like the vat. The vat smells like Mauld. The arrangement works.

sharedEdits.what: Register the Forgekeep Concord client pack (loom/tanning vat/gem bench/organ actions, new fletch combos, artisan NPCs, reward exchange).
sharedEdits.snippet: import './forgekeep';            // Forgekeep Concord — artisan quarter (smithing/crafting/fletching expansion)

sharedEdits.what: Self-registering server domain for Forgekeep Concord (hard-leather tanning at the vat + the maker's-mark/shard reward exchange). Add next to the other intent imports (after `import './intent-misc';`).
sharedEdits.snippet: import './intent-forgekeep'; // side-effect: registers the 'forgekeep' domain (tan-hard + reward exchange)

verdictApplied: flax NOT redefined — referenced as an existing item (input to resonance_thread).
orikon_platebody bumped from Smith 98 to 99 so it does not undercut rune_platebody's level (verdict balance flag #1).
All bows/crossbows emit `attackSpeed` (the real field), not `speed` (verdict balance flag re: field name).
Jewelry rows are station:null, so the engine's thread auto-fold (server/intents.ts) means each jewelry craft also consumes 1 thread — accepted, identical to existing gold/sapphire/ruby jewelry.
studded_body/studded_chaps are station:'loom' (non-null) so NO thread is auto-folded — they consume the body/chaps + 15 steel studs only, as the spec intends.
High tiers (orikon/dawnsteel) are deterministic — no successChance, matching rune.
cuts: master_chisel/true_hammer PERKS (10% faster gemcut / 10% bar save) — engine hooks do not exist in make()/gemcut and are outside this expansion's file ownership; the items ship as reward-shop prestige tools, perks deferred to the engine owner.
Commission board / daily make-X tasks for makers_mark — needs new persistent-task engine work; instead Quartermaster Doram exchanges 5 resonant shards -> 1 maker's mark so marks are obtainable today.
Per-tier crossbow stocks (oak stock / yew stock) — collapsed to the single wooden_stock (carved from oak_logs) for all three crossbows; the spec's own item list only defined one stock item.
hardleather currently has no consuming recipe (the spec's studded line consumes leather_body/leather_chaps); it ships as the tan-hard output and a future crafting input.
crossTeamDeps: orikon_ore is defined HERE (the Mining fragment did not ship it); Halle's brokerage is its only source until the Mining deep pits add it to rock tables.
uncut_diamond / uncut_dragonstone are defined HERE; sources are Doram's reward exchange (dragonstone) and future gem-rock/drop tables (diamond) — Yael's shop sells lower uncuts meanwhile.
silver jewelry (silver_ring/silver_amulet) consumes the Mining expansion's EXISTING silver_bar (smelt 20) — not redefined here.
